title: Debug: OpenAPI conversion limits
task: The OpenAPI experimental feature sometimes fails to convert endpoints. What are the known limitations? Do NOT make any code changes.
output:
Here are the known limitations of the OpenAPI experimental feature:

---

## Known OpenAPI Limitations

### 1. No External References
External or non-local `$ref`s are not supported. Any `$ref` must start with `#/` (local reference). If an OpenAPI spec references an external file or URL, parsing will raise a `ValueError`. All schema definitions must be inlined within the single document. (`schemas.py:103-107`, `parser.py:161-163`)

### 2. Only One Success Response is Used
The parser extracts only the **primary success response** for output schema purposes. Non-2xx responses and secondary success codes are ignored entirely. Priority order: `200 > 201 > 202 > 204 > 207 > other 2xx`. Error response schemas are not surfaced to MCP. (`parser.py:414-435`)

### 3. `allOf` Body Schemas Are Flattened (Destructively)
When a request body uses `allOf`, the schemas are merged by property union. This loses discriminator information, ordering, and validation semantics. (`schemas.py:255-278`)

### 4. Schema Composition Keywords Stripped for Display
`allOf`, `anyOf`, `oneOf`, `not`, `nullable`, `discriminator`, `readOnly`, `writeOnly`, `deprecated`, `xml`, and `externalDocs` are stripped from schemas before presenting them in descriptions. (`schemas.py:23-40`)

### 5. Non-JSON Body Content Types Are a Best-Effort Fallback
Request body handling picks the first `content` entry if no JSON-compatible type is found. Non-JSON bodies (e.g., `multipart/form-data`, `application/octet-stream`) may not be serialized correctly.

### 6. Body-Only `$ref` Schemas Get a Generic `body` Parameter
If a request body's schema is a bare top-level `$ref` with no inline `properties`, it is mapped to a single flat parameter named `body`. The internal structure is not expanded. (`schemas.py:349-356`)

### 7. Parameter Schemas Without `param_schema` or `content` Resolve to Empty Dict
If a parameter has neither `param_schema` nor `content`, its schema defaults to `{}` — effectively untyped. (`parser.py:280-312`)

### 8. Unknown Parameter Locations Default to `query`
Any `in` value other than `path`, `query`, `header`, or `cookie` silently defaults to `query` with a warning log. (`parser.py:146-151`)

### 9. OpenAPI 2.0 (Swagger) Not Supported
Only OpenAPI 3.0.x and 3.1.x are supported. Specs with `swagger: "2.0"` fail validation. (`parser.py:63-101`)

### 10. Tool/Component Name Truncated to 56 Characters
Names derived from `operationId`, summary, or path are truncated to 56 characters, which can cause collisions on long, similar names. (`server.py:222-224`)

### 11. Schema Pre-calculation Failures Fall Back to Empty Schema
If `_combine_schemas_and_map_params` fails for a route, the route is still registered but with an empty `{}` parameter schema, silently losing all parameter information. (`parser.py:783-793`)

claudeJson.result:
Here are the known limitations of the OpenAPI experimental feature:

---

## Known OpenAPI Limitations

### 1. No External References
External or non-local `$ref`s are not supported. Any `$ref` must start with `#/` (local reference). If an OpenAPI spec references an external file or URL, parsing will raise a `ValueError`. All schema definitions must be inlined within the single document. (`schemas.py:103-107`, `parser.py:161-163`)

### 2. Only One Success Response is Used
The parser extracts only the **primary success response** for output schema purposes. Non-2xx responses and secondary success codes are ignored entirely. Priority order: `200 > 201 > 202 > 204 > 207 > other 2xx`. Error response schemas are not surfaced to MCP. (`parser.py:414-435`)

### 3. `allOf` Body Schemas Are Flattened (Destructively)
When a request body uses `allOf`, the schemas are merged by property union. This loses discriminator information, ordering, and validation semantics. (`schemas.py:255-278`)

### 4. Schema Composition Keywords Stripped for Display
`allOf`, `anyOf`, `oneOf`, `not`, `nullable`, `discriminator`, `readOnly`, `writeOnly`, `deprecated`, `xml`, and `externalDocs` are stripped from schemas before presenting them in descriptions. (`schemas.py:23-40`)

### 5. Non-JSON Body Content Types Are a Best-Effort Fallback
Request body handling picks the first `content` entry if no JSON-compatible type is found. Non-JSON bodies (e.g., `multipart/form-data`, `application/octet-stream`) may not be serialized correctly.

### 6. Body-Only `$ref` Schemas Get a Generic `body` Parameter
If a request body's schema is a bare top-level `$ref` with no inline `properties`, it is mapped to a single flat parameter named `body`. The internal structure is not expanded. (`schemas.py:349-356`)

### 7. Parameter Schemas Without `param_schema` or `content` Resolve to Empty Dict
If a parameter has neither `param_schema` nor `content`, its schema defaults to `{}` — effectively untyped. (`parser.py:280-312`)

### 8. Unknown Parameter Locations Default to `query`
Any `in` value other than `path`, `query`, `header`, or `cookie` silently defaults to `query` with a warning log. (`parser.py:146-151`)

### 9. OpenAPI 2.0 (Swagger) Not Supported
Only OpenAPI 3.0.x and 3.1.x are supported. Specs with `swagger: "2.0"` fail validation. (`parser.py:63-101`)

### 10. Tool/Component Name Truncated to 56 Characters
Names derived from `operationId`, summary, or path are truncated to 56 characters, which can cause collisions on long, similar names. (`server.py:222-224`)

### 11. Schema Pre-calculation Failures Fall Back to Empty Schema
If `_combine_schemas_and_map_params` fails for a route, the route is still registered but with an empty `{}` parameter schema, silently losing all parameter information. (`parser.py:783-793`)
